steps.title: Identify the hidden assumption
steps.content: The student's logic depends on a single unstated assumption: that 120 meters in New Caledonia and 170 meters in Fiji experience temperature differences based only on depth. Specifically, the student assumes that shallower depth automatically means warmer water across all ocean regions, but this ignores the possibility of regional temperature variation.

steps.title: Test that assumption
steps.content: Option B directly undercuts it. If Fiji waters are systematically warmer than New Caledonian waters at every given depth, then 170 meters in Fiji could easily be as warm as or warmer than 120 meters in New Caledonia—falsifying the hypothesis. The other options either describe post-hatching migration (A), provide an alternative explanation for depth choice based on habitat (C), or actually reinforce the assumption (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
